poem:
**Wavering MPs**

This morning the paper says  
No. 10 releases a memo,  
like a secret recipe pinned to the fridge  
to prove the cook didn't lie about the soup.

Brown appears, an elder in a long coat,  
urging unity while wars flicker offshore.

Meanwhile phones ring—  
half a dozen calls,  
a rain of persuasion  
on 165 seats.
prompt: You are the poet Billy Collins. You woke up this morning feeling creative. You have just read this story in the newspaper: "No 10 publishes previously confidential memo to refute claim that Starmer misled MPs over Mandelson appointment – as it happened 5.49pm BST Afternoon summary Gordon Brown, the former prime minister, has urged Labour MPs to support Keir Starmer when the Commons votes tomorrow on a Tory motion saying the privileges committee should investigate allegations Starmer lied to MPs about the Peter Mandelson vetting process. Brown issued a statement after the speaker, Lindsay Hoyle, announced that a vote will take place tomorrow. Brown said: At challenging times both for our country and the world, the Labour party, has always sought to put the needs of the country first and with conflicts raging around the world with profound consequences for our country, this is the time to do so. Whatever the parliamentary games at Westminster, what the country expects of everyone in Labour is to focus on the priorities of the British people, which is what Keir Starmer is doing and for which he deserves all our support. Labour has a working majority of 165, and the government should be able to vote down the Tory motion (which is based on very flimsy reasoning anyway – see 1.32pm) without difficulty. But, with Starmer’s authority with his MPs already diminished, No 10 is taking no chances. This is from the Times’ Steven Swinford: Wavering MPs are being bombarded with calls from ministers and Starmer allies, sometimes half a dozen in quick succession The PM is addressing PLP tonight Gordon Brown has rowed in". Write a poem in YOUR OWN DISTINCTIVE STYLE, no more than 60 words long. You may add a one line title at the top if you like.
news: No 10 publishes previously confidential memo to refute claim that Starmer misled MPs over Mandelson appointment – as it happened 5.49pm BST Afternoon summary Gordon Brown, the former prime minister, has urged Labour MPs to support Keir Starmer when the Commons votes tomorrow on a Tory motion saying the privileges committee should investigate allegations Starmer lied to MPs about the Peter Mandelson vetting process. Brown issued a statement after the speaker, Lindsay Hoyle, announced that a vote will take place tomorrow. Brown said: At challenging times both for our country and the world, the Labour party, has always sought to put the needs of the country first and with conflicts raging around the world with profound consequences for our country, this is the time to do so. Whatever the parliamentary games at Westminster, what the country expects of everyone in Labour is to focus on the priorities of the British people, which is what Keir Starmer is doing and for which he deserves all our support. Labour has a working majority of 165, and the government should be able to vote down the Tory motion (which is based on very flimsy reasoning anyway – see 1.32pm) without difficulty. But, with Starmer’s authority with his MPs already diminished, No 10 is taking no chances. This is from the Times’ Steven Swinford: Wavering MPs are being bombarded with calls from ministers and Starmer allies, sometimes half a dozen in quick succession The PM is addressing PLP tonight Gordon Brown has rowed in
